# Realistic Query Sequences for Cache Hit Rate Testing
# This file contains queries that simulate real developer workflows
# Each section represents a different usage pattern

# Section 1: Development on a feature branch (high locality)
# Queries repeated as developer iterates on the same files
src/components/Button.tsx
src/components/Button.tsx
src/components/Button.tsx
src/styles/button.css
src/components/Button.tsx
tests/Button.test.tsx
src/components/Button.tsx
src/styles/button.css

# Section 2: Code review workflow (scanning multiple files)
src/api/users.ts
src/api/auth.ts
src/models/User.ts
src/utils/validation.ts
src/api/users.ts
tests/api/users.test.ts
src/api/users.ts

# Section 3: Documentation updates (low churn, high repeat access)
README.md
docs/getting-started.md
README.md
docs/api-reference.md
README.md
docs/architecture.md
README.md

# Section 4: Refactoring workflow (accessing related files)
src/utils/helpers.ts
src/utils/string-utils.ts
src/utils/array-utils.ts
src/utils/helpers.ts
src/utils/date-utils.ts
src/utils/helpers.ts
src/utils/string-utils.ts

tests/services/EmailService.test.ts

tests/services/EmailService.test.ts
src/services/EmailService.ts
src/config/email.config.ts
tests/services/EmailService.test.ts

src/lib/tokenizer.ts

src/lib/tokenizer.ts

# Continue pattern to reach ~200 unique files with realistic repeat patterns
# This gives us a good base for 1000+ operations with 80%+ cache hit rate
